c74object.name: tap.convolve~
c74object.description:
The tap.convolve~ object convolves a stereo signal with an impulse response (IR) stored in a buffer~. Because the IR can be as long as a reverb tail, it uses uniformly-partitioned overlap-save (UPOLS) FFT convolution: the IR is split into equal partitions, each transformed once, and the output is formed by a frequency-domain multiply-accumulate over a delay line of past input spectra. It is fully self-contained — no pfft~ host is required. Latency equals one blocksize.
It is true stereo: four IR paths describe the full 2×2 response (outL = inL∗h_LL + inR∗h_RL, outR = inL∗h_LR + inR∗h_RR). How the four paths are read from the bound buffer~ depends on its channel count — 4+ channels give a full true-stereo IR (ch1=LL, ch2=LR, ch3=RL, ch4=RR), 2 channels a plain stereo IR with no cross-feed (ch1=LL, ch2=RR), and 1 channel a mono IR on both diagonals (the stereo image is preserved). Send a set message to bind a different buffer~; the IR is (re)analysed off the audio thread and swapped in without dropping out.

objarg.name: buffer
objarg.description: Name of the buffer~ holding the impulse response.

objarg.name: blocksize
objarg.description: FFT partition size in samples (a power of two). This is also the processing latency [default: 512].

method.name: signal
method.description: The left and right input signals to be convolved with the impulse response (left and right inlets).

method.name: set
method.description: Bind the object to a named buffer~ and (re)load its contents as the impulse response.

method.name: clear
method.description: Flush the reverb tail — clears the internal input history and any pending output. The loaded IR is kept.

attribute.name: mix
attribute.description: Dry/wet mix as a percentage: 0 = dry input only, 100 = fully wet convolution [default: 100].

attribute.name: gain
attribute.description: Output gain in dB, applied to the wet+dry mix [default: 0 dB].

attribute.name: predelay
attribute.description: Pre-delay in milliseconds applied to the wet (convolved) signal, delaying the onset of the reverb relative to the dry signal (0–2000) [default: 0 ms].

attribute.name: normalize
attribute.description: When on, energy-normalises the impulse response so its overall level is consistent regardless of the IR's loudness or length [default: 1 (on)].

attribute.name: blocksize
attribute.description: FFT partition size in samples (a power of two, 16–8192). This is also the processing latency; larger values are more efficient for long IRs. Changes take effect when the DSP chain is next started [default: 512].

attribute.name: maxsize
attribute.description: Maximum impulse-response length in samples that can be loaded; longer IRs are truncated. Changes take effect when the DSP chain is next started, and larger values use more memory [default: 131072].
